label: Uncomplicated cystitis in a nonpregnant patient
inclusion_criteria: Lower urinary tract symptoms are most consistent with bladder infection, such as burning with urination, frequency, urgency, or suprapubic discomfort.
No fever, flank pain, vomiting, sepsis concern, or clinician concern for kidney infection.
Patient can take oral medicines and maintain hydration.
No urologic procedure, catheter-associated infection, or complicated UTI pathway is needed at discharge.
exclusion_criteria: Male patient or patient requiring sex-specific complicated UTI pathway.
Fever, flank pain, rigors, vomiting, or concern for pyelonephritis.
Sepsis, immunocompromise, kidney transplant, urinary obstruction, or indwelling catheter.
Pelvic inflammatory disease, sexually transmitted infection, kidney stone, or abdominal emergency concern.
must_not_miss: Pelvic infection or STI when symptoms suggest it.
default_follow_up: Primary care, clinic, or urgent care follow-up in 2 to 3 days if symptoms are not improving, sooner if symptoms worsen.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. CDC and MedlinePlus support UTI framing, antibiotic treatment when clinically indicated, hydration, and pyelonephritis or sepsis return precautions.

source_audit.notes: CDC and MedlinePlus support UTI framing, antibiotic treatment when clinically indicated, hydration, and pyelonephritis or sepsis return precautions.
